title: Shadow attestation (v2)
description: Frozen schema for Shadow's Ed25519-signed attestation records. Field set is closed as of Shadow v2.0.0. New signed fields require schema_version 3 and a back-compat test. All future needs are expected to flow into the `extensions` map, which is signed as an opaque blob.

spec_version.description: Wire-format tag. Kept as v1 because the append-only pattern is preserved from v1; schema_version tracks the schema-freeze generation.

schema_version.description: Bumped when the set of signed fields changes. v2 freezes the field set enumerated below. Any new top-level field requires bumping to 3 with a documented migration.

mode.description: Signing mode. hmac is symmetric (dev / back-compat). ed25519 is asymmetric (procurement-recommended).

signature.description: Base64url-encoded signature. Length depends on mode.

request_commitment.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonicalized request body.

output_commitment.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonicalized response body (attestation field stripped).

model_id.description: The exact model id used (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6). Detects silent model substitution.

completed_at_utc.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp of decision completion.

key_id.description: Public identifier of the signing key. Used for key rotation.

previous_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the previous attestation's own hash. Enables cross-vertical hash chain. Null for the head of a chain.

dictionary_hash.description: SHA-256 of the reason-code dictionary in force at decision time. Detects post-hoc dictionary edits (v1.5.8+).

citation_registry_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the citation registry. Detects silent regulatory-citation changes (v1.5.18+).

proxy_schema_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the ECOA §701 protected-class proxy schema. Detects post-hoc softening of the blocklist (v1.5.19+).

original_content_hash.description: SHA-256 of the pre-summarization original content when CCR (compressed content retrieval) is used. Bank counsel can verify the summary derives from what the bank actually saw (v1.5.20+).

policy_invariance_score_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the Judge Card (per arXiv:2605.06161 Policy Invariance Score). Detects post-hoc metric relaxation (v1.5.23+).

adverse_action_notice_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the GAICF-compatible adverse-action notice text (per arXiv:2607.04103 layer 3). Detects post-hoc softening of the notice (v1.5.24+).

sampling_seed_commitment_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the sampling seed committed BEFORE deliberation (per arXiv:2606.16121). Detects 'regenerate until we get the answer we want' pattern (v1.5.28+).

evidence_partition_scheme_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the evidence-partition scheme (per arXiv:2607.01661). Detects silent partition-scheme swap between decisions (v1.5.30+).

heterogeneity_commitment_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the heterogeneous-debate enforcement commitment (per arXiv:2606.19826). Detects post-hoc relaxation of min-providers requirement (v1.5.32+).

claim_type_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the Pramana typed-claim envelope (per arXiv:2605.20312). Detects silent post-hoc reclassification of claim type (v1.5.37+).

bian_coverage_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the BIAN v9 service-domain coverage matrix (v1.5.39+).

eticas_taxonomy_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the Eticas AI Risk Taxonomy v2.0.0 mapping (per arXiv:2607.02201, v1.5.40+).

sive_fixture_set_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the SIVE fixture-set commitment (per arXiv:2607.00910, v1.5.41+).

calibration_ranking_split_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the Bayesian calibration-vs-ranking split (per arXiv:2605.27712, v1.5.42+).

extensions.description: Signed opaque map for future needs. Consumers should treat unknown extension keys as advisory. Adding a well-known key here does NOT bump schema_version. Adding a new top-level field DOES.

signed_fields_v2.description: Enumerates the exact field names (as they appear in the wire object) that participate in the signing payload. lib/attestation.js:_signingPayload MUST accept exactly these keys (camelCase parameter shape) plus the required core. CI cross-checks this list against the code.
